execution_overview.overall_summary: Built a Python Docker environment and executed both planned logistic regression analyses using the original Benin2012survey.csv dataset. Task1 required a minor code repair to compute the LR test manually; after the fix, both tasks ran successfully. Results show a positive and statistically significant interaction for the wife(FON) condition among Fon coethnics and a significant LR test for adding interaction terms.

deviations.description: Replaced use of results.compare_lr_test with a manual LR test computation using model log-likelihoods and scipy.stats.chi2.
deviations.reason: statsmodels' GLMResults does not expose compare_lr_test; the original call caused an AttributeError.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: Followed the planned logistic regression with interaction; only the LR test implementation was repaired.

test_statistics.other: LR test for adding interaction terms: LR=9.883, df=2, p=0.00714

result_raw.result_text: The interaction C(condition)[T.wife(FON)]:C(coethnic)[T.co-ethnic(FON)] is positive and statistically significant (b=1.583, SE=0.549, z=2.88, p=0.00396).

conclusion.conclusion_text: Results support the focal claim: mentioning a Fon wife significantly increases support among Fon coethnics relative to non-coethnics (positive, significant interaction).

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: Executed the planned logistic regression with interaction using the same filtering and variable construction as Task1.

result_raw.result_text: The interaction C(condition)[T.wife(FON)]:C(coethnic)[T.co-ethnic(FON)] is positive and statistically significant (b=1.583, SE=0.549, z=2.88, p=0.00396).

conclusion.conclusion_text: The comparable analysis corroborates the claim: the wife(FON) condition increases support among Fon coethnics (positive, significant interaction).
